input.counterSource: CSS-Tricks "Masonry Layout is Now grid-lanes" (December 2025) confirms the syntax transition; MDN primary source at the cited URL corroborates `display: grid-lanes` and Level 3 attribution.
input.evidence: The claim is accurate on all counts. (1) The supporting MDN quote "Level 3 of the CSS grid layout specification defines masonry layout (also known as grid-lanes layout)" directly supports both the spec level and the grid-lanes name — no overreach. (2) The current CSS syntax IS `display: grid-lanes` (not the older `grid-template-rows: masonry`): the CSSWG formally resolved this change on January 31, 2025, and CSS-Tricks confirmed the `grid-lanes` keyword was adopted by December 2025. (3) The MDN page at the cited URL now documents `display: grid-lanes` as the official syntax, consistent with the claim. (4) The CSS Grid Layout Module Level 3 Working Draft (drafts.csswg.org/css-grid-3/#grid-lanes-model) is the authoritative primary source — high quality for this claim. (5) The information is current: the spec change happened in 2025, the claim reflects post-January-2025 state. One nuance: the older `grid-template-rows: masonry` approach was the prior experimental syntax (shipped behind flags in Firefox/Safari), but the claim explicitly contrasts against it and correctly identifies `display: grid-lanes` as the current Level 3 definition. No contradicting credible evidence found.
